name: cadrianmae-claude-marketplace
description: Personal marketplace for academic workflow automation, session management, and development productivity tools. Includes tracking, documentation generation, audio feedback, and integration utilities.

plugins.name: session
plugins.description: Flat session management system with .current-session tracking. Adapted from iannuttall/claude-sessions. Includes commands for starting, ending, updating, listing, and resuming development sessions.

plugins.name: context
plugins.description: Hierarchical parent-child session context handoff system. Pass context between sessions using /context:send and /context:receive. Auto-creates /tmp/claude-ctx/ directory. Supports optional subject and custom paths. Files use pattern: /tmp/claude-ctx/ctx-{direction}-{subject}.md

plugins.name: track
plugins.description: Automatic reference and prompt tracking via hooks for academic work. Prompts tracked via Stop hook with LLM summaries, sources tracked via PostToolUse hook with ASCII format. Export to bibliography, methodology, BibTeX, timeline.

plugins.name: datetime
plugins.description: Natural language date/time parsing and calculations using native GNU date command. Parse expressions like 'tomorrow', 'next week', '3 days', calculate date differences, get current date/time. Zero dependencies. Includes auto-invoked skill for proactive temporal awareness.

plugins.name: code-pointer
plugins.description: Claude-only skill that opens files in VSCode at specific line and column positions. Auto-invoked when explaining code, debugging, pointing to TODOs, or guiding users to exact file locations. Uses VSCode CLI with progressive disclosure reference docs.

plugins.name: pandoc
plugins.description: Document conversion toolkit for Pandoc with templates, validation, and format-specific helpers. Convert markdown to PDF, DOCX, HTML, and presentations with smart defaults and YAML frontmatter validation. Includes academic paper templates, BibTeX examples, and Makefile for automation.

plugins.name: cadrianmae-integration
plugins.description: Integrate marketplace plugins into CLAUDE.md using @ imports. Scans installed plugins, generates documentation with skills and commands, creates marketplace-plugins.md, and adds import to user-level or project-level CLAUDE.md. Uses /integrate command with scope selection.

plugins.name: cadrianmae-issues
plugins.description: File bugs and feature requests for marketplace plugins as GitHub issues on cadrianmae/claude-marketplace via gh CLI. Use /cadrianmae-issues:bug and /cadrianmae-issues:feature.

plugins.name: gencast
plugins.description: Auto-invoke gencast to generate podcasts from documents. Always uses --minimal flag. Supports all CLI options: planning, styles, audiences, voices, dialogue/plan saving.

plugins.name: tool-docs
plugins.description: Documentation agents for command-line tools (pandoc, postgres, git, docker). Provides pandoc-guide agent for expert documentation lookup with WebFetch integration and tool-lookup skill for offline quick references. Auto-triggers on tool queries.

plugins.name: nvr
plugins.description: Neovim Remote integration for Claude Code. Unified /nvr command with open, list, status, workspace subcommands. Git-root-aware socket discovery.

plugins.name: cron
plugins.description: Hook-based scheduled notifications using crontab(5) syntax. Anacron-style catch-up with per-tick deduplication. Global and per-project schedules with add/replace modes. Single unified interactive /cron command. Requires python3 and jq.

plugins.name: tts
plugins.description: Piper-based text-to-speech for Claude Code. Speaks assistant responses aloud via a Stop hook; interrupt-on-type via a UserPromptSubmit hook. Three speak modes (full/truncate/summary via Haiku). Multi-speaker voice support via name:speaker syntax. Single unified interactive /tts command. Requires Linux, PipeWire (paplay), and piper binary with voices at ~/.local/share/piper-voices/.

plugins.name: audio-feedback
plugins.description: Audio feedback for Claude Code hook events. Plays short synth sounds on response complete, notifications, context compaction, user input, and more. Per-event configuration with theme support. Requires Linux + PipeWire (paplay).

plugins.name: formalizer
plugins.description: Rewrite text into a different tone or register (21 tones: professional, academic, concise, empathetic, diplomatic, Irish English, etc.) with optional 1-5 intensity anchored to Joos's Five Clocks. Skill for inline rewrites, isolated agents for batch/compare, iterative refinement, and sequential pipeline chains.
